stories.title: Income Tax Liabilities Statistics: Tax Year 2023 to 2024
stories.summary: HMRC published its latest income tax liabilities statistics based on the annual Survey of Personal Incomes, covering projections through to the 2026-27 tax year.

stories.title: Green-Led Council Plans to Ban Cooperation with Home Office on Immigration Raids
stories.summary: A Green-led London council is planning to ban its officials from working with the Home Office on immigration raids, in a move that signals growing local resistance to central government immigration enforcement.

stories.title: UK Appoints Kate Harrisson as New HMTC for China and Hong Kong
stories.summary: Kate Harrisson has been appointed His Majesty's Trade Commissioner (HMTC) for China and Hong Kong, taking up the role in September. She joins from the FCDO where she currently serves as Director for the Asia Pacific.

stories.title: UK Government Launches Campaign to Prevent Methanol Poisoning Tragedies Abroad
stories.summary: The UK government launched a campaign with survivors and families urging British travellers to 'Know the Signs' of methanol poisoning following high-profile tragedies abroad.

stories.title: Young People in Port Talbot in Line for Up to £5m Boost in Jobs
stories.summary: The Department for Work and Pensions announced up to £5 million in funding to help young people in Port Talbot find, prepare for and secure new well-paid jobs.

stories.title: Agency Agreement on Compensation Recovery Services for Scotland
stories.summary: DWP formalised an agency agreement with Scottish Ministers for delivery of Compensation Recovery services in Scotland, clarifying jurisdictional responsibilities.

stories.title: Government Secures the Future of Dartmoor's Iconic Ponies
stories.summary: DEFRA announced that Dartmoor ponies will be removed from stocking rate calculations in new Environmental Management agreements, protecting them from potential culling pressures faced by farmers.

stories.title: DEFRA Annual Report and Accounts 2025 to 2026 Published
stories.summary: DEFRA published its annual report and accounts setting out progress in delivering its priorities and outcomes alongside planned expenditure for 2025-26.

stories.title: First British-Made Artillery Barrels Delivered to Ukraine
stories.summary: The first deliveries of British-made artillery barrels have been made to Ukraine under a £61 million contract, supporting hundreds of UK defence industry jobs.

stories.title: Ministry of Defence Annual Report and Accounts 2025 to 2026 Laid in Parliament
stories.summary: The MOD's annual report and accounts for 2025-2026 was laid before Parliament on 15 July 2026, outlining the department's performance and expenditure.

stories.title: UKDI Unifies Innovation to Accelerate Frontline Capabilities and Drive National Growth
stories.summary: UK Defence Innovation (UKDI) has been announced as the focal point for defence innovation, helping UK companies rapidly develop and scale cutting-edge prototypes for frontline use.

stories.title: North of England Unites for First-Ever Joint Trade Mission to India
stories.summary: Northern Mayors joined forces for a landmark joint trade mission to India as DBT reports UK-India trade reached £47.9 billion in 2025, a 10% increase on the previous year.

stories.title: DBT Annual Report and Accounts 2025 to 2026 Published
stories.summary: The Department for Business and Trade published its annual report with detailed commentary on performance, governance arrangements and departmental expenditure for 2025-26.

agencies.name: DSIT
agencies.description: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

stories.title: Seven Non-Executive Members Appointed to Information Commission Board
stories.summary: Seven Non-Executive Directors have been appointed to the Information Commission Board, supporting a transition to a new board-led governance model later this year, with chair recruitment also launching.

stories.title: Children's Wellbeing Online: Social Media Research Published
stories.summary: DSIT published research into parents' and children's views on social media use, potential restrictions, and the possible introduction of an under-16 social media ban.

stories.title: ARIA Annual Report and Accounts 2025 to 2026
stories.summary: The Advanced Research and Invention Agency (ARIA) published its third Annual Report and Accounts, covering the year to 31 March 2026 under the sponsorship of DSIT.

stories.title: Thousands of Women Could Be Spared Painful Cancer Exam by New NHS AI Blood Test
stories.summary: NHS England announced a new AI-powered blood test that could potentially spare thousands of women from undergoing painful cancer examinations, representing a significant diagnostic breakthrough.

stories.title: NHS England Publishes Quality Strategy to Make Quality the Organising Principle
stories.summary: NHS England published a quality strategy on 15 July 2026 outlining a structured approach to 'making quality the organising principle for all NHS activity' in England.

stories.title: Newborn Screening for SMA to Begin Across 72% of England from October 2026
stories.summary: NHS England confirmed that newborn screening for Spinal Muscular Atrophy (SMA) will begin across 72% of England from October 2026 as part of an in-service evaluation programme.
